channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Parents push for change after sexual assault case at child care center
item.description: "Three years later, you're still having to go to court, or get therapy. So it's not just it happens and then it's over with," Amber Chatelain said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats narrowly win a Pennsylvania Senate seat, scoring an upset in Republican-leaning suburbs
item.description: MANHEIM, Pa. (AP) — Democrat James Andrew Malone narrowly won a special election for a Pennsylvania state Senate seat in a stretch of Republican-leaning suburbs and farming communities, scoring an upset in a territory that a Democrat hasn’t represented in the chamber for 136 years.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 4 American soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have died, NATO leader says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, but that he did not yet know the details. A U.S. official would say only that the four soldiers were involved in a training accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices on Wednesday unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, and ordered him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: ICE detains Tufts University student amid Trump admin's campus crackdown
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement has detained Tufts University student Rumeysa Ozturk in Somerville, Massachusetts, according to the agency and a statement the school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NFL player charged after 190 dogs seized from Oklahoma dog fighting venture
item.description: An Oklahoma man is facing federal charges after officials seized 190 dogs from an animal fighting venture, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa death: Footage from outside home released
item.description: The footage suggests that one of the top priorities for responding deputies was the couple's privacy.
item.dc:creator: Annalisa Pardo, NewsNation

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats narrowly win a Pennsylvania Senate seat, scoring an upset in Republican-leaning suburbs
item.description: Democrat James Andrew Malone narrowly won a special election for a Pennsylvania state Senate seat in a stretch of Republican-leaning suburbs and farming communities.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, KRON4

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 30 years after music icon Selena's murder, Yolanda Saldívar is up for parole. Here's what to know
item.description: Thirty years ago, Latino music legend Selena Quintanilla-Pérez was killed by her fan club’s president, Yolanda Saldívar.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KREX 5

item.title: Former Duke football player confesses to killing ex-real estate agent friend in her upscale townhome: police
item.description: Former Duke University football player Brandon Braxton has allegedly confessed to killing his former high school friend and Charlotte, North Carolina, real estate agent Whitney Hurd.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kelly, Gallego call on Hegseth to resign over war plans Signal chat
item.description: Sens. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) and Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) called on Wednesday for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign after The Atlantic revealed texts he sent on the Signal group chat used to lay out plans for attacks against the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Waterbury woman accused of holding stepson captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: The Waterbury woman accused of holding her stepson captive for most of his life briefly appeared in court Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jayne Chacko, NewsNation

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A new Chili’s near Scranton will be a throwback to 'The Office,' 'awesome blossom' and all
item.description: It has been nearly two decades since the workplace mockumentary "The Office” first set an episode inside a Chili's, where Michael Scott handed out Dundie awards to his ever-tolerant employees, including trophies for the whitest sneakers and for stinking up the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dollar Tree selling Family Dollar to a pair of private equity firms for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree is selling Family Dollar to a pair of private equity firms for $1 billion after unsuccessfully trying to incorporate the bargain chain for a decade.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business News, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Americans get new look at the illegal gangbangers Trump deported and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bureau of Indian Affairs Missing and Murdered Unit investigating alleged military base killing
item.description: The Bureau of Indian Affairs Missing and Murdered Unit aids in the arrest, and indictment of an active-duty airman accused of murdering a 21-year-old tribal woman at an Air Force base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger trial: Idaho weather from night of student murders clouds 'moon and stars' alibi
item.description: Idaho prosecutors want to use National Weather Service records in Bryan Kohberger's student murders trial that show fog, clouds and a night unfit for stargazing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: (KTLA) -- The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday. The team released a statement Tuesday afternoon that said it would visit the White House "in keeping with long-standing baseball tradition.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director says Israeli soldiers beat him after attack by settlers
item.description: HEBRON, West Bank (AP) — Only a few weeks ago, Hamdan Ballal stood on a stage in Los Angeles accepting an Oscar for the film “No Other Land,” a documentary depicting his West Bank village’s struggle against Israel’s occupation.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's nominee to run NIH
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed health researcher Jay Bhattacharya as the next leader of the National Institutes of Health (NIH). Bhattacharya was confirmed on a party- line vote, 53 to 47.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Transgender Americans aim to block Trump's passport policy change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When Ash Lazarus Orr went to renew his passport in early January, the transgender organizer figured it would be relatively routine. But more than two months on, Orr is waiting to get a new passport with a name change and a sex designation reflecting who he is.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DHS suspends green card processing for refugees, asylees
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said it is pausing review of green card applications for some refugees and asylees, leaving in limbo those who came to the U.S. after fleeing unrest. The directive from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Mega Millions is about to change: 7 things to know
item.description: Frequently play the Mega Millions? The national lottery jackpot game is about to be overhauled with the promise of bigger prizes, better odds and faster-growing jackpots — if you don't mind paying a higher ticket price.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Michigan couple held in Mexican prison, daughter says
item.description: They couple was arrested on March 4 at an airport in Cancun and have been held without bond for 21 days, the daughter claims.
item.dc:creator: Taryn Simmons, KRON4

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Couple held at Mexico jail after allegedly defrauding Cancun hotel out of more than $100K
item.description: This story has been updated with additional information. LANSING, Mich. (WLNS) — Paul and Christy Akeo, a couple from Spring Arbor in Jackson County, are currently being held in a maximum-security Mexican prison, their family tells 6 News.
item.dc:creator: Taryn Simmons, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chick-fil-A unveils new item you can't find in restaurants
item.description: Chick-fil-A is rolling out a new bottled dressing that fans won’t find at any of its 3,100 U.S. restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Abortion once more plays a key role in a state political fight, this time in Wisconsin's court race
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — As the candidates for a Wisconsin Supreme Court seat squared off in a recent debate before early voting, one issue came up first and dominated at the start. “Let’s talk about abortion rights,” the moderator said.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4
